title: Master the New Contact Detail Page in GoHighLevel — Boost Agency Productivity
description: Master GoHighLevel's new Contact Detail Page with Auto-Save, keyboard navigation & collapsible panels. Boost agency productivity instantly.
html_content:
If you're running a digital marketing agency, you know the friction: switching between tabs, hunting for contact history, losing context on client interactions. Your team is losing hours every week just navigating your CRM.

GoHighLevel's new Contact Detail Page is built to eliminate that waste. It's a redesigned, lightning-fast interface that puts every customer interaction—calls, texts, emails, tasks, notes—in one unified view. In this guide, I'll walk you through how to access it, master its layout, and use productivity features like Auto-Save and keyboard navigation to boost your entire agency's efficiency.

Ready to experience it yourself? Get a free 30-day trial of GoHighLevel and see why thousands of agencies run their entire business on one platform.

📋 In This Guide

  • How to Access the New Contact Detail Page in Labs
  • Understanding the Three-Panel Layout Design
  • Leveraging Auto-Save to Streamline Data Entry
  • Using Collapsible Panels to Reduce Clutter
  • Mastering Keyboard Navigation Shortcuts
  • Real-World Workflow Improvements for Agencies

How to Access the New Contact Detail Page in Labs

The new Contact Detail Page isn't automatically enabled for all accounts—it lives in GoHighLevel's Labs feature, which means you're testing cutting-edge improvements before they roll out to everyone.

Here's how to turn it on:

  1. Log into your GoHighLevel account
  2. Navigate to Settings (bottom left of your sidebar)
  3. Scroll to find Labs or Beta Features
  4. Look for "New Contact Detail Page" and toggle it ON
  5. Refresh your browser or navigate back to Contacts
  6. Click on any contact—you'll see the redesigned interface

The moment you enable it, you'll feel the difference. The interface loads faster, the panels are reorganized for logic flow, and every action is designed to keep your hands on the keyboard instead of hunting for buttons.

💡 Pro Tip

If you manage multiple sub-accounts or agency clients, enable the new Contact Detail Page across all accounts at once. The consistency will speed up your team's adoption and reduce confusion during the transition.

Understanding the Three-Panel Layout Design

The new Contact Detail Page reorganizes information into three intelligent panels: Left, Center, and Right. This isn't random—it's designed around how your team actually works.

Left Panel: Contact Core Data

This is your contact's hub. You'll find their name, phone, email, tags, and custom fields here. It's lightweight and always visible, so you never lose context on who you're talking to. The left panel stays put while you scroll through activity on the right.

Center Panel: Activity Timeline

Every interaction lives here: calls, SMS, emails, tasks, notes, and pipeline changes. The timeline is chronological and searchable, so finding a conversation from two weeks ago takes seconds, not minutes. This is where your team spends most of their time.

Right Panel: Quick Actions & Forms

Send a text, log a call, assign a task, or update a custom field without leaving the page. The right panel adapts based on what you're doing—it's contextual and always relevant. When you close an action, it collapses automatically.

Together, these three panels create a unified command center. You're not jumping between tabs; everything you need is visible at once.

Leveraging Auto-Save to Streamline Data Entry

One of the most frustrating moments in any CRM is when you forget to save—and your data is lost. GoHighLevel's new Auto-Save eliminates that risk entirely.

How Auto-Save works:

  • Every change you make to contact fields is automatically saved in real-time
  • No "Save" button to click—no more mental overhead
  • The system debounces requests, so you're not hammering the server with constant updates
  • You see a subtle confirmation indicator when data is synced

For agencies managing dozens of contacts per day, this is a game-changer. Your team stops worrying about whether they saved their work and focuses entirely on managing the relationship. When you're updating custom fields, adding notes, or changing contact status, it just works.

This is especially powerful when you have team members collaborating on a single contact. If Sarah updates a custom field and Mark adds a note seconds later, both changes stick—no conflicts, no overwrites.

Using Collapsible Panels to Reduce Clutter

Information overload is real. A contact with years of history, dozens of custom fields, and multiple ongoing conversations can feel chaotic. That's why collapsible panels exist.

In the new Contact Detail Page, every section can collapse: activity types (calls, emails, tasks), custom field groups, and related data. You decide what matters right now and hide the rest.

Best practices for collapsing:

  • During initial outreach: Keep only Communication history and Tasks visible—hide Pipeline and Custom Fields until they're relevant
  • During proposal/negotiation: Expand Deal Pipeline and related documents; collapse old activity
  • During onboarding: Show Custom Fields for setup data, Tasks for deliverables, hide earlier communication

Your team can also save their collapse preferences as a personal layout. Once you configure your view, GoHighLevel remembers it for your next contact.

Mastering Keyboard Navigation Shortcuts

If your team is still reaching for the mouse constantly, they're losing productivity with every click. The new Contact Detail Page supports instant keyboard navigation—and it changes the speed game entirely.

Essential shortcuts:

  • Tab: Move between fields and buttons in sequence
  • Shift + Tab: Move backward through fields
  • Enter: Confirm actions, submit forms, open expanded views
  • Escape: Close modals, collapse panels, return to timeline
  • Cmd/Ctrl + S: Manually save (though Auto-Save usually handles this)

For power users, keyboard-first workflows cut contact navigation time by 30-40%. Imagine your team opening a contact, updating two custom fields, adding a task, and logging a call—all without touching the mouse. That's the efficiency we're talking about.

💡 Pro Tip

Create a quick reference guide for your team: print the keyboard shortcuts and post them at workstations for the first two weeks. Habits stick faster when shortcuts are visible.

Real-World Workflow Improvements for Agencies

These features aren't just incremental improvements—they compound into massive time savings across your entire team.

Scenario 1: Lead Qualification
Your team member opens a new lead, reviews the last three interactions (collapsed activity), tags them appropriately, and assigns a follow-up task—all in 45 seconds instead of 2 minutes. With 20 leads per day, that's 21 minutes saved daily. Over a month, that's 7 hours of reclaimed productivity.

Scenario 2: Multi-Team Collaboration
Your sales rep and account manager both work on the same contact. Auto-Save means there's no risk of overwrites or lost updates. The contact data stays fresh and accurate in real-time.

Scenario 3: Bulk Contact Management
When you're moving contacts through your pipeline at scale—say, 50 clients per week—the speed gains multiply. Keyboard navigation + Auto-Save + collapsible panels means your team processes contacts faster and with fewer errors.

For a 10-person agency managing 500+ active contacts, these improvements translate to roughly 10-15 hours of reclaimed time per week. That's nearly a full-time person's worth of productivity.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I enable the new Contact Detail Page if I don't see Labs in my Settings?

Labs is available to all GoHighLevel users, but it's usually in Settings under a "Labs" or "Beta Features" section. If you can't find it, contact GoHighLevel support or ensure you're on the latest version of the platform. You may need to log out and back in.

Can my entire team use the new Contact Detail Page, or is it just for me?

Once you enable it in Labs, every team member with access to your GoHighLevel account can use it. It's account-wide, so all your users get the same modern interface. Individual keyboard shortcut preferences are personal, but the core layout is consistent across your team.

Will my data be lost if I switch back to the old Contact page?

No. The old and new Contact Detail Pages pull from the same database. If you toggle the new page off in Labs, your contact data remains untouched. You can switch back and forth without any risk of losing information.

Does Auto-Save work offline?

Auto-Save requires an internet connection to sync changes to GoHighLevel's servers. If you lose connection, your changes are held locally and synced automatically when your connection returns. Your browser will show an offline indicator if you're disconnected.

Can I customize which panels appear in the new Contact Detail Page?

Yes. You can collapse and expand any panel, and GoHighLevel remembers your preferences. However, you cannot completely hide panels like the Left or Center—they're core to the layout. You can only collapse sections within them.
